title: Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located along the Western Ghats and spread across 74 square kilometres (29 mi2) in Chakkittapara and Koorachundu revenue villages of
overview.description: Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located along the Western Ghats and spread across 74 square kilometres (29 mi2) in Chakkittapara and Koorachundu revenue villages of Koyilandy Taluk in Kozhikode, Kerala. The sanctuary is part of the western ghats, a biodiversity hotspot. It also comes under the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve and forms a part of the Wayanad Elephant Reserve.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Koyilandy
topPlaces.description: Koyilandy (IPA: [kojilɐːɳɖi];) is a municipality and a taluk in Kozhikode district, Kerala on the Malabar Coast. The historical town is located right in the middle of the coast of Kozhikode district, between Kozhikode and Kannur, on National Highway 66.

topPlaces.name: Viyyur (Koyilandy taluk)
topPlaces.description: Viyyur is one of the 34 villages in Koyilandy thaluk of Kozhikode district.

topPlaces.name: Chengottukavu
topPlaces.description: Chengottukavu is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Pisharikavu
topPlaces.description: Pisharikavu Temple is a temple of the Hindu goddess Bhadrakali at Kollam, Koyilandy, Kozhikode district, Kerala state of south India.

topPlaces.name: Nambrathkara
topPlaces.description: Nambrathkara is situated 4 km away from Koyilandy in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Keezhariyur
topPlaces.description: Keezhariyur is a village near Koyilandy in Kozhikode district, that lies on the eastern shore of Akalapuzha Lake. This village is famous for the bomb case happened during Independence struggle.

topPlaces.name: Moodadi
topPlaces.description: Moodadi is a village in Koyilandy taluk of Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Muchukunnu
topPlaces.description: Muchukunnu is a small village on the Malabar Coast of south-western India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (141 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Koyilandy, Viyyur (Koyilandy taluk), Chengottukavu, Pisharikavu, Nambrathkara.
